items.title: India’s energy security amid conflicts
items.summary: The conflict in West Asia has shown how geopolitical shocks can transmit to India’s domestic economy, given that the country imports over 85% of its crude needs. In a fragmented energy market, India’s edge lies not in self-sufficiency but in optionality.

It’s not necessary. We’re not looking for ‌a fight.” Mr. Hegseth said ​during a briefing.

items.title: Das Adam Smith Problem: rethinking Smith’s moral and economic worlds
items.summary: The Das Adam Smith Problem shows an apparent dichotomy between sympathy and self-interest in Adam Smith’s works, but most scholars now view it as a misunderstanding, arguing that his philosophy forms a coherent whole uniting ethics and economics
